conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something you used to manage, like cooking, how does that make you think about yourself?
I think about how I used to be the one helping others, and now I can barely take care of myself. It's hard to accept that I'm not that person anymore.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It’s been building over the past year, but it really hit me hard a few months ago when I turned 40. It’s like I reached this milestone and suddenly realized I don't have the same life I used to.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Everything feels hollow. I used to have a routine, but now I just exist. I don't have any direction or purpose.
When you say that, is it more about wanting to withdraw from everything, or feeling like you don't want to be here?
I don't think I want to be here anymore. I feel like I've been waiting for something to happen, and it hasn't. I don't see a reason to keep going like this.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
Honestly, I don't see them unfolding at all. I just see a blank page.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It's a mix of both, I think. I'm just so tired of fighting to get out of bed every day.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I think people would be better off. I'm just taking up space and not contributing anything.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to find the motivation to do stuff during the day?
Definitely. I just feel so drained. Even the simple things, like going to the gym, seem like too much effort.
You mentioned thinking about things you could have done differently—what kinds of things come to mind?
Just stuff from the past, like work or relationships. I keep thinking that if I had done things differently, maybe I wouldn't be in this situation now.
When you think about being in this situation now, what does that look like for you?
It looks like I failed. I keep thinking that I should be further along by now, and I’m just stuck here.
When you say you feel stuck, what does that feel like day to day?
It’s like I’m just going through the motions. I do things, but I don’t actually feel like I’m moving forward.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things could get better, or does it seem like you'll stay stuck?
I don’t really know. I just feel like I’m running out of time, and I’m not sure how to make things better.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started a few months ago, maybe around the time my relationship ended. It just kept getting worse, and now it feels like it’s all I can think about.
Has this feeling of being stuck made you more irritable or on edge with people around you?
Yeah, definitely. I just feel like everyone else has their lives together, and I’m over here struggling to get out of bed.
You mentioned struggling to get out of bed—what does a typical morning look like for you now?
It’s hard. I wake up, and I just lie there for a while. I don’t want to get up and face the day because I know I won't be able to handle it.
